assertions.reason: The targeted edge-worker restore command passed cleanly, and the covered tests directly verify that persisted runtime state restores repository associations through explicit session data and the global registry instead of repo-first storage assumptions.
evidence: Command `pnpm --filter cyrus-edge-worker exec vitest run test/EdgeWorker.missing-session-recovery.test.ts test/GlobalSessionRegistry.test.ts test/AgentSessionManager.repository-associations.test.ts` exited 0 with 3 test files passed and 63 tests passed.

`packages/edge-worker/test/EdgeWorker.missing-session-recovery.test.ts` lines 685-771 define persisted runtime restoration coverage, including `should restore zero-association sessions into the global registry without repo buckets`, which restores `agentSessionsById` state and expects `restoredSession?.repositoryAssociations` to equal `[]` after `restoreMappings(state)`.

`packages/edge-worker/test/GlobalSessionRegistry.test.ts` lines 222-238 verify lookups come from explicit `repositoryAssociations`, and lines 458-514 verify `restoreState(...)` plus serialize/restore round-trip behavior in the global session registry.

`packages/edge-worker/test/AgentSessionManager.repository-associations.test.ts` lines 64-130 restore a session containing `repositoryAssociations: [{ repositoryId: "repo-restore", associationOrigin: "restored", status: "selected" }]` and then assert the global registry still exposes that restored association.

commandsRun.observation: Vitest passed for the targeted restore surface: GlobalSessionRegistry (46 tests), AgentSessionManager.repository-associations (2 tests), and EdgeWorker.missing-session-recovery (15 tests); 63/63 tests passed in 1.02s.

summary: VAL-RUNTIME-004 passed on the targeted edge-worker restore surface: the restore-focused Vitest command succeeded, and the cited tests show repository associations are restored from explicit persisted session data through the global registry rather than repo-first storage buckets.
